comment_text.sn35.228:1.5: Compare Pañcaviṁśa Brāhmaṇa 6.4.7: “‘Hail to the ocean! Hail to the ocean’s eye!’ he says. For speech is truly the ocean, and mind is the ocean’s eye.”

root_text.sn35.228:2.1: Yo taṁ rūpamayaṁ vegaṁ sahati, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, atari cakkhusamuddaṁ saūmiṁ sāvaṭṭaṁ sagāhaṁ sarakkhasaṁ;
root_text.sn35.228:2.5: Yo taṁ rasamayaṁ vegaṁ sahati, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, atari jivhāsamuddaṁ saūmiṁ sāvaṭṭaṁ sagāhaṁ sarakkhasaṁ;
root_text.sn35.228:2.9: Yo taṁ dhammamayaṁ vegaṁ sahati, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, atari manosamuddaṁ saūmiṁ sāvaṭṭaṁ sagāhaṁ sarakkhasaṁ;

translation_text.sn35.228:1.1: “Mendicants, an unlearned ordinary person speaks of the ocean.
translation_text.sn35.228:1.2: But that’s not the ocean in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.sn35.228:1.3: That’s just a large body of water, a large sea of water.
translation_text.sn35.228:1.4: For a person, the eye is an ocean,
translation_text.sn35.228:1.5: and its currents are made of sights.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.1: Someone who can prevail over those currents is said to have crossed over the ocean of the eye, with its waves and whirlpools, its saltwater crocodiles and monsters.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.2: Crossed over, gone to the far shore, the brahmin stands on solid ground.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.3: For a person, the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.228:2.8: and its currents are made of ideas.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.9: Someone who can prevail over those currents is said to have crossed over the ocean of the mind, with its waves and whirlpools, its saltwater crocodiles and monsters.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.10: Crossed over, gone to the far shore, the brahmin stands on solid ground.”
translation_text.sn35.228:2.11: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn35.228:2.12: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn35.228:3.1: “A knowledge master who’s crossed <j>the ocean so hard to cross,
translation_text.sn35.228:3.2: with its saltwater crocodiles and monsters, <j>its waves, whirlpools, and dangers;
translation_text.sn35.228:3.3: they’ve completed the spiritual journey, <j>and gone to the end of the world,
translation_text.sn35.228:3.4: they’re called ‘one who has gone beyond’.”
